title: Non-commissioned officers and other ranks
description: All engineering technologists and technicians, except drafters, not listed separately. Test the safety of structures, vehicles, or vessels using x-ray, ultrasound, fiber optic or related equipment. Build, install, test, or maintain optical or fiber optic equipment, such as lasers, lenses, or mirrors, using spectrometers, interferometers, or related equipment. (Based on 24 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Adjust and repair assistive devices and make adaptive changes to other equipment and environments.
Provide sample analysis results to physicians to assist in diagnosis and report progress, attitudes, attendance, and accomplishments of patients.
Train other medical personnel in phlebotomy or laboratory techniques and assist occupational therapists in administering therapy programs.
Transport specimens or fluid samples from collection sites to laboratories and document collection routes.
Maintain records of inventory or equipment usage and order medical instruments or supplies as necessary.
Receive, store, and inventory pharmaceutical supplies or medications, checking for expiration and low stock inventory levels.
Document patient, specimen, insurance, or billing information into the computer system and prepare or maintain treatment records.
Collect fluid or tissue samples using appropriate collection procedures and explain these procedures to patients.
Transport patients to and from treatment areas or units using wheelchairs or stretchers, providing necessary assistance.
Observe patients during treatment to compile data on their responses and progress, reporting findings to medical staff.
Perform clerical, administrative, and secretarial duties such as answering phones, scheduling appointments, and ordering supplies.
Maintain and clean equipment, work areas, or other supplies, ensuring instruments are sterile and ready for use.
Assist patients with daily living activities to promote independence and facilitate therapeutic goals.
Secure patients in therapy equipment and monitor their treatment to ensure safety and effectiveness.
Clean and disinfect equipment or supplies, including endoscopic instruments and blood-drawing tools, adhering to safety protocols.
Participate in training sessions to enhance professional skills and keep abreast of developments in the field.
Assist speech-language pathologists in the conduct of client screenings, assessments, or developing treatment plans.
Test or maintain equipment such as photonic devices to ensure correct performance and functionality.
Collect and compile data to document clients' progress toward meeting established treatment objectives.
Conduct safety checks to verify proper equipment functioning and assist with the management of medical emergencies.
Dispose of biohazardous fluids, sharps, and other waste materials, following applicable laws and safety standards.
Document procedures, such as calibration, methods, processes, or results of optical or fiber optic equipment and non-destructive testing (NDT).
Assist scientists or engineers in photonic experiments and in the development of new products, fixtures, tools, or processes.
Adjust or maintain equipment including lasers, laser systems, microscopes, and related measurement devices.
Visually examine materials or components for signs of corrosion, metal fatigue, cracks, or other flaws using various tools.
Prepare reports on non-destructive testing results and interpret or evaluate test results in accordance with applicable codes or standards.
Conduct liquid penetrant tests to locate cracks, and identify defects in solid materials using ultrasonic and thermal testing techniques.
Make radiographic images and produce images of objects on film to detect flaws while leaving objects intact.
Examine structures or vehicles using non-destructive testing techniques and map imperfections within objects using sonic measurements.
Develop or use new non-destructive testing methods like acoustic emission testing or thermal testing, and test optomechanical or optoelectrical products.
Supervise or direct the work of non-destructive testing trainees or staff and monitor inventory levels for necessary supplies.

Order medical supplies for healthcare facilities or laboratories.
Operate drones for remote inspection of large or hard-to-reach structures, such as wind turbines, bridges, or tall buildings.
